DifficultyModes.description: Five difficulty modes selected at campaign creation. Cannot be changed mid-campaign.

modes.name: Easy
effects: After every battle, select a crew member to receive +1 XP
When setting up a tabletop battle, if you would face 5+ opponents, remove one Basic enemy
Only the Play 20 campaign turns and Win 20 tabletop battles Victory Conditions can be completed

modes.name: Normal
effects: No changes to game mechanics. All rules apply as written.

modes.name: Challenging
effects: When rolling 2D6 to determine enemy numbers faced in battle, if either of the dice score a 1 or 2, reroll them before selecting the highest die

modes.name: Hardcore
effects: Add an additional Basic enemy to every battle
Apply a -2 penalty to all Seize the Initiative rolls
Add +1 when rolling for Unique Individuals
Begin the campaign with one less story point

modes.name: Insanity
effects: Add an additional Specialist enemy to every battle
Apply a -3 penalty to all Seize the Initiative rolls
The opposing side always includes a Unique Individual, even for Roving Threats
Roll 2D6. On an 11-12, they include two Unique Individuals
Receive no Stars of the Story options
Receive no story points. If an item or event would grant them, it has no effect, and the item or event is not rerolled

DifficultyToggles._note: Individual toggles layered on any difficulty mode. See data/compendium/difficulty_toggles.json for full implementation data.

categories.name: Strength-adjusted Enemies
categories.description: Enemy count equals crew size plus modifiers instead of rolling

categories.name: Time is Running Out
sub_options: They are Paying us by the Hour
There's Movement all over the Place

categories.name: Reduced Lethality
categories.description: Exempt one character from post-battle injury roll when 2+ injured

ProgressiveDifficulty._note: See data/progressive_difficulty.json for full turn-based milestone tables.

options.name: Option 1: Classic
options.description: Simple respawn + strength increases by campaign turn. Can be combined with Option 2.

options.name: Option 2: Compendium
options.description: Gradually enables difficulty toggles and elite-level enemies by campaign turn. Can be combined with Option 1.

EliteLevelEnemies._note: Upgraded enemy tables with stronger stats, better composition, and slightly improved rewards. NOT a difficulty mode — a toggle or progressive milestone.
